title: Search Insert Position
description:
Given a sorted array of distinct integers `nums` and a `target`, return the
index if the target is found. If not, return the index where it would be
inserted to keep the array sorted. Aim for `O(log n)`.

solutions.summary: Binary search; the failed search converges on the insertion point.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Bound the search range
steps.explanation: `left`/`right` enclose the range; `left` is also the eventual insert point.

code.python:
def search_insert(nums, target):
    left = 0
    right = len(nums) - 1
    return left

steps.title: Loop while the range is valid
steps.explanation: Inspect the middle of the current range each step.

steps.title: Return on an exact hit
steps.explanation: If the middle equals the target, that index is the answer.

steps.title: Move past smaller values
steps.explanation: If the middle is below the target, search the right half.

steps.title: Discard larger values
steps.explanation: Otherwise search the left half. When the loop ends, `left` is the insert point.

solutions.summary: Return the first index whose value is at least the target.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Scan for the first big-enough value
steps.explanation: The first index with `nums[i] >= target` is where it belongs.
